checklist_item.title: Does compute_fork_version always return a valid 4-byte Version for all epochs >= 0?

reachability.notes: A null fork version would crash P2P message handling.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the compute_fork_version function implementation. 2. Review all code paths to verify they return a valid 4-byte Version. 3. Test with edge-case epoch values: epoch=0, epoch=MAX_UINT64, epoch at fork transition boundaries. 4. Verify no code path returns null, undefined, or error for valid epoch inputs. 5. Check that fork configuration covers all possible epoch ranges without gaps. 6. Attempt to trigger function failure by providing epochs that fall between defined fork boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-12, Invariant: p2p-SG-001-INV-003. This is an internal invariant - a null or invalid return would crash the node during P2P handshake or gossip validation. Falsification approach: find edge-case epoch values that cause function to return error/null/undefined.

checklist_item.title: Can the slot modulo index into proposer_lookahead exceed valid bounds or map to an incorrect epoch proposer?

reachability.notes: An off-by-one in the modulo operation could cause an out-of-bounds access or map to the wrong slot's proposer.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all code locations where `slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH` is computed as an index into `state.proposer_lookahead`. 2. Verify the modulo operation uses the correct divisor (SLOTS_PER_EPOCH, not SLOTS_PER_EPOCH + 1 or similar). 3. Test boundary conditions: slot values at epoch boundaries (slot 0, slot SLOTS_PER_EPOCH-1, slot SLOTS_PER_EPOCH, slot 2*SLOTS_PER_EPOCH-1). 4. Verify the computed index is always in range [0, SLOTS_PER_EPOCH) by checking array access bounds. 5. Confirm no off-by-one errors exist in the index calculation that could cause out-of-bounds access. 6. Trace the proposer assignment lookup to ensure the index correctly corresponds to the current epoch's proposer schedule.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-37, Invariant: FULU-SG005-INV-002. Pre-condition verification: The modulo operation must guarantee index bounds. An off-by-one could cause array out-of-bounds crash (DoS) or assign the wrong proposer to a slot causing missed blocks or conflicting proposals.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Is sidecar.index validated to be within [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) before processing?

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft a sidecar with an out-of-range index and send it via P2P gossip.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the verify_data_column_sidecar function in the gossip validation pipeline. 2. Review the validation logic that checks sidecar.index against NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS. 3. Craft a malicious DataColumnSidecar with index = NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS and send via P2P gossip. 4. Craft a malicious DataColumnSidecar with index = NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS + 1 and send via P2P gossip. 5. Craft a malicious DataColumnSidecar with index = MAX_UINT64 and send via P2P gossip. 6. Verify that all out-of-range sidecar messages are rejected at the gossip validation layer. 7. Confirm no array out-of-bounds access, node crash, or data corruption occurs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-13, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-002-INV-001. Boundary property with attacker-controlled input - elevated to Critical severity.

checklist_item.title: Verify Gossip Network Actor: Does the gossip network properly propagate rejection of invalid column indices?

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft a sidecar with an out-of-range index and send it via P2P gossip.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the gossip network actor's handling of DataColumnSidecar messages. 2. Verify that invalid sidecars are not propagated to other peers. 3. Check that the node does not penalize the sender peer correctly for invalid messages. 4. Ensure no partial processing occurs before rejection. 5. Verify that rejection happens early in the validation pipeline to minimize resource consumption.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-13, Supporting node check for actor-gossip-network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Beacon Node Actor: Is the beacon node protected from array out-of-bounds access when processing column indices?

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft a sidecar with an out-of-range index and send it via P2P gossip.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all locations in the beacon node where sidecar.index is used as an array index. 2. Verify bounds checking occurs before any array access. 3. Check for off-by-one errors in the comparison (< vs <=). 4. Verify that NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS constant is correctly defined and matches spec. 5. Test with fuzzing to ensure no edge cases allow out-of-bounds access. 6. Verify that a crash or panic cannot be triggered by malformed input.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-13, Supporting node check for actor-beacon-node. Array out-of-bounds can lead to crash, data corruption, or potentially code execution.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Does verify_data_column_sidecar reject sidecars with empty kzg_commitments?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with empty commitments and send via gossip.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the verify_data_column_sidecar function in the gossip validation pipeline. 2. Identify where kzg_commitments length is validated. 3. Craft a test sidecar with kzg_commitments as an empty list/array. 4. Submit the malformed sidecar via the P2P gossip network interface. 5. Verify that the sidecar is rejected with an appropriate error (REJECT or IGNORE). 6. Confirm no downstream processing occurs (check for division-by-zero paths or unexpected state changes). 7. Review all code paths that consume kzg_commitments to ensure they handle empty arrays defensively.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-14, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-002-INV-002. Empty commitments could cause division-by-zero or unexpected behavior in downstream processing. PoC required: empty-commitment sidecar bypassing validation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Gossip Network Node Integrity: Are all data column sidecars from gossip network validated for non-empty kzg_commitments before processing by beacon node?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with empty commitments and send via gossip.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Map the data flow from actor-gossip-network to actor-beacon-node. 2. Identify all entry points where data column sidecars cross the trust boundary (tb-001). 3. Verify that validation occurs BEFORE any processing by the beacon node. 4. Check that no code paths bypass the validation layer. 5. Test with fuzzing: send malformed sidecars with various empty/null kzg_commitments values. 6. Verify that rejected sidecars do not cause resource exhaustion or state corruption.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-14, Supporting check for nodes: actor-gossip-network, actor-beacon-node. Ensures internal logic properly supports boundary security.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Does verify_data_column_sidecar reject sidecars with excessive KZG commitments beyond epoch-specific max_blobs_per_block limit?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with excessive commitments. The epoch-dependent limit adds complexity.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the verify_data_column_sidecar function implementation. 2. Trace how get_blob_parameters(epoch) derives max_blobs_per_block for a given epoch. 3. Verify the validation check exists: len(kzg_commitments) > max_blobs_per_block must result in rejection. 4. Test boundary conditions: submit sidecar with exactly max_blobs_per_block commitments (should accept), then max_blobs_per_block + 1 (must reject). 5. Test epoch boundary scenarios: craft sidecars at slots where blob parameters change between epochs. 6. Verify rejection response does not leak internal state or cause resource exhaustion before rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-15, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-002-INV-003. CRITICAL: Epoch boundaries where blob parameters change are especially high-risk attack surfaces. Bug bounty eligible per source property.

checklist_item.title: Verify Gossip Network Node (actor-gossip-network): Is the P2P gossip layer properly validating sidecar commitment counts before forwarding?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with excessive commitments. The epoch-dependent limit adds complexity.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review gossip message validation at the network layer. 2. Confirm that sidecars with excessive commitments are rejected at the gossip level before being propagated. 3. Verify that malformed sidecars do not cause the gossip peer to be scored negatively or disconnected inappropriately. 4. Test that a flood of invalid sidecars does not overwhelm the validation pipeline.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-15, Supporting node check for actor-gossip-network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Beacon Node (actor-beacon-node): Does the beacon node correctly derive epoch from sidecar slot and apply the correct blob parameters?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with excessive commitments. The epoch-dependent limit adds complexity.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review the slot-to-epoch derivation logic used in verify_data_column_sidecar. 2. Verify get_blob_parameters returns correct max_blobs_per_block for each epoch. 3. Test with sidecars at epoch boundary slots (last slot of epoch N vs first slot of epoch N+1). 4. If blob parameters differ between epochs, verify the correct epoch's parameters are applied. 5. Check for off-by-one errors in epoch calculation that could apply wrong limits.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-15, Supporting node check for actor-beacon-node. The epoch-dependent nature of blob parameters introduces complexity that could lead to consensus issues if clients interpret epochs differently.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does verify_data_column_sidecar reject sidecars with mismatched array lengths (column, kzg_commitments, kzg_proofs)?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with mismatched array lengths.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the verify_data_column_sidecar function in the gossip validation pipeline. 2. Locate where column, kzg_commitments, and kzg_proofs arrays are accessed. 3. Verify that length equality checks occur BEFORE any array indexing operations. 4. Craft test sidecars with: (a) len(column) > len(kzg_commitments), (b) len(column) < len(kzg_proofs), (c) len(kzg_commitments) != len(kzg_proofs). 5. Submit each malformed sidecar via P2P gossip and verify immediate rejection with appropriate error. 6. Confirm no out-of-bounds memory access occurs (use sanitizers/fuzzing). 7. Check that rejection happens at gossip layer, not deeper in processing.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-16, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-002-INV-004. Mismatched lengths could cause out-of-bounds access during KZG proof verification, crashing the node.

checklist_item.title: Verify Array Length Validation Order: Are length checks performed before any array element access in KZG verification?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft sidecar with mismatched array lengths.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the code path from sidecar receipt to KZG proof verification. 2. Identify all locations where column[i], kzg_commitments[i], or kzg_proofs[i] are accessed. 3. For each access, verify a bounds check or length equality assertion precedes it. 4. Check for TOCTOU issues where length could change between check and use. 5. Verify error handling does not leak information about internal array sizes.
checklist_item.notes: Supporting check for PROP-W2B3-16. Ensures validation order prevents exploitation window.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-008: Are cell indices correctly constructed to match column index in KZG proof verification?

reachability.notes: Incorrect cell index assignment would cause KZG verification to pass for data at wrong positions.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs function implementation. 2. Verify that cell_indices is constructed as [CellIndex(sidecar.index)] * len(sidecar.column). 3. Check that all cells in a column share the same cell index equal to the column index. 4. Attempt to submit a DataColumnSidecar via P2P with mismatched cell indices and verify rejection. 5. Test with cell indices pointing to wrong column positions and confirm KZG verification fails. 6. Verify that no code path allows cell indices to diverge from the column index.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-17, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Invariant: p2p-SG-003-INV-001. Critical: Incorrect cell index mapping would break data availability guarantees.

checklist_item.title: Verify KZG Library Cell Index Handling: Does the KZG library correctly process cell indices for column verification?

reachability.notes: Incorrect cell index assignment would cause KZG verification to pass for data at wrong positions.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the KZG library interface used for cell/column verification (actor-kzg-library). 2. Trace how cell_indices are passed from verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs to the KZG library. 3. Verify the KZG library validates that provided cell indices match expected column positions. 4. Test with crafted inputs where cell indices are valid but point to wrong positions. 5. Confirm that position mismatch causes proof verification to fail. 6. Check for any bypass conditions where index validation is skipped.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-17, Supporting Node: actor-kzg-library. KZG proof verification is the critical validation layer.

checklist_item.title: Verify Beacon Node Column Index Consistency: Does the beacon node maintain correct column-to-cell-index mapping?

reachability.notes: Incorrect cell index assignment would cause KZG verification to pass for data at wrong positions.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the DataColumnSidecar handling path in the beacon node (actor-beacon-node). 2. Verify that sidecar.index is correctly used to construct cell indices. 3. Check that the column length (len(sidecar.column)) matches the expected number of cells. 4. Test with P2P messages containing DataColumnSidecars where sidecar.index is manipulated. 5. Verify the beacon node rejects sidecars with inconsistent index/column relationships. 6. Audit for off-by-one errors in index calculation or array sizing.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-17, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node. Invariant: All cells in column must share same cell index equal to column index.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-008: Are verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch input arrays validated for consistent lengths before KZG library invocation?

reachability.notes: Inconsistent array lengths passed to KZG library could cause undefined behavior.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all call sites invoking verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch from P2P message handlers. 2. Trace data flow from P2P ingress to KZG library call. 3. Verify that len(commitments_bytes) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs_bytes) is enforced BEFORE calling the KZG library. 4. Craft malicious P2P messages with inconsistent array lengths (e.g., 10 commitments, 5 cells, 8 proofs). 5. Confirm that malformed inputs are rejected at the beacon node layer, NOT at the KZG library. 6. Check for off-by-one errors in length validation logic. 7. Verify error handling does not leak sensitive state on rejection.
checklist_item.falsification_approach: Attempt to bypass length validation by sending P2P messages where array lengths differ (e.g., commitments_bytes has N elements while cells has N-1). If the KZG library receives inconsistent arrays, the pre-condition is falsified.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-18, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Source Invariant: p2p-SG-003-INV-002. This is a boundary property where untrusted P2P data crosses into the trusted KZG verification library. The pre-condition ensures defensive validation occurs at the trust boundary before invoking cryptographic primitives.

checklist_item.title: Can the KZG_COMMITMENTS_INCLUSION_PROOF_DEPTH invariant (depth=4) be violated, causing Merkle proof verification failures or acceptance of invalid proofs?

reachability.notes: Incorrect proof depth would cause all inclusion proofs to fail or accept invalid proofs.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the constant definition for KZG_COMMITMENTS_INCLUSION_PROOF_DEPTH and verify it equals 4. 2. Identify all code paths that use this constant for Merkle proof generation and verification. 3. Attempt to construct a valid Merkle proof with depth != 4 and verify it is rejected. 4. Attempt to construct a Merkle proof at depth 4 with forged kzg_commitments and verify the inclusion proof fails. 5. Trace the proof verification logic to ensure depth is validated before proof acceptance. 6. Check for any off-by-one errors in proof depth calculations that could allow proofs of incorrect depth to pass validation.

falsification_test.objective: Violate the invariant that KZG_COMMITMENTS_INCLUSION_PROOF_DEPTH must equal 4
falsification_test.approach: State transition attack - attempt to force proof verification with mismatched depth
attack_vectors: Submit a sidecar with a Merkle proof of depth 3 or 5 and observe if it passes validation
Manipulate intermediate proof nodes to simulate incorrect tree depth
Check if proof depth is hardcoded vs. dynamically validated
falsification_test.expected_safe_behavior: All proofs with depth != 4 should be rejected immediately
failure_indicators: Proof with incorrect depth is accepted
Valid sidecar with correct depth is rejected
Proof verification crashes or hangs on edge-case depths

checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-19, Invariant: p2p-SG-004-INV-001, Primary Element: p2p-SG-004, Covered Nodes: actor-beacon-node. Wrong proof depth would cause valid sidecars to be rejected (liveness failure) or invalid sidecars accepted (safety failure).

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-002: Is the Merkle inclusion proof correctly validated to link kzg_commitments hash to BeaconBlockBody root?

reachability.notes: Attacker could craft sidecar with forged inclusion proof.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the P2P message handler that receives blob sidecars. 2. Locate the is_valid_merkle_branch function and verify it is called for all incoming sidecars. 3. Verify the generalized index used matches the blob_kzg_commitments field position in BeaconBlockBody. 4. Craft a sidecar with: (a) valid kzg_commitments but incorrect Merkle proof, (b) invalid kzg_commitments with a forged proof targeting the expected root, (c) proof with incorrect generalized index. 5. Confirm all malformed sidecars are rejected before further processing. 6. Check for edge cases: empty commitments, maximum commitments, boundary index values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-20, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Source Invariant: p2p-SG-004-INV-002. Requires PoC showing forged inclusion proof bypassing is_valid_merkle_branch.

checklist_item.title: Verify Beacon Node rejects sidecars with invalid Merkle inclusion proofs at P2P ingress

reachability.notes: Attacker could craft sidecar with forged inclusion proof.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the code path from P2P message receipt to Merkle proof validation in the beacon node. 2. Verify there is no code path that skips proof verification. 3. Check that proof verification occurs BEFORE the sidecar is stored, forwarded, or processed. 4. Test with a peer sending crafted sidecars with: (a) truncated proof, (b) proof with wrong depth, (c) proof with sibling hashes in wrong order. 5. Verify the beacon node disconnects or penalizes peers sending invalid proofs. 6. Check logging and metrics for proof validation failures.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-20, Supporting Node: actor-beacon-node. Data availability integrity depends on correct proof validation at ingress.

checklist_item.title: Verify Block Proposer generates correct Merkle inclusion proofs for blob sidecars

reachability.notes: Attacker could craft sidecar with forged inclusion proof.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the code path where block proposer constructs blob sidecars with inclusion proofs. 2. Verify the generalized index computation matches the SSZ spec for blob_kzg_commitments field. 3. Verify hash_tree_root is computed correctly over sidecar.kzg_commitments. 4. Test proof generation with: (a) single blob, (b) maximum blobs per block, (c) varying commitment sizes. 5. Validate generated proofs against a reference implementation. 6. Check that proof generation uses the same BeaconBlockBody root that will be signed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-20, Supporting Node: actor-block-proposer. Incorrect proof generation would cause valid blocks to be rejected.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-010: Does compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar always return valid subnet indices within [0, 128)?

reachability.notes: Subnet miscalculation would fragment the gossip network.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar function implementation. 2. Enumerate all valid column_index values (typically 0 to NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS-1). 3. For each valid column_index, verify the returned subnet value is in range [0, 128). 4. Test boundary conditions: column_index = 0, column_index = max_valid_index. 5. Test edge cases around modular arithmetic to ensure no off-by-one errors. 6. Verify that DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT is correctly defined as 128. 7. Check for integer overflow/underflow in subnet computation. 8. Validate that sidecars are actually published to the computed subnet during integration testing.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-21, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Invariant: p2p-SG-005-INV-001,INV-002. Incorrect subnet computation causes sidecars to be published to wrong subnets, fragmenting data availability and potentially causing consensus failures.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-001: Are ALL 13 REJECT validation checks enforced before data_column_sidecar gossip messages are accepted?

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all 13 REJECT validation checks in the data_column_sidecar gossip validation pipeline. 2. For each REJECT check, craft a malformed sidecar that should trigger rejection. 3. Verify that each malformed sidecar is properly rejected and NOT forwarded to peers. 4. Test edge cases: empty fields, maximum size boundaries, invalid cryptographic proofs, out-of-range indices. 5. Verify ordering dependencies between checks - ensure no check can be bypassed by triggering an earlier IGNORE. 6. Test concurrent message handling to ensure race conditions cannot bypass validation. 7. Confirm that partial validation failures result in complete message rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-006-INV-001. This is a CRITICAL boundary check - any single REJECT bypass allows network-wide propagation of invalid sidecars.

checklist_item.title: Verify Gossip Network Actor (actor-gossip-network): Can an external attacker inject sidecars that bypass validation at the network boundary?

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the exact code path where gossip messages enter the system from the P2P network layer. 2. Review message deserialization for potential type confusion or buffer overflow vulnerabilities. 3. Verify that malformed gossip envelopes are rejected before validation pipeline processing. 4. Check for differences in validation between direct peer connections and relayed messages. 5. Test rate limiting and resource exhaustion protections at the gossip network boundary. 6. Verify that rejected messages do not leak information to attackers (timing, error messages).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Supporting node check for actor-gossip-network. Focus on untrusted data entering from external peers.

checklist_item.title: Verify Beacon Node Actor (actor-beacon-node): Does the beacon node correctly enforce all REJECT conditions before forwarding sidecars?

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the message flow from gossip receipt to peer forwarding within the beacon node. 2. Identify the exact function(s) implementing each of the 13 REJECT checks. 3. Verify that the forwarding decision is made ONLY after ALL checks pass. 4. Check for any code paths that could forward messages before validation completes (async handling bugs). 5. Review state dependencies - ensure validation uses correct current state (head block, current slot). 6. Test behavior when beacon node is syncing - verify validation is not relaxed. 7. Check for TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) vulnerabilities between validation and forwarding.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Supporting node check for actor-beacon-node. The beacon node is the trust boundary enforcer - any logic error here compromises network integrity.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the active validator set correctly filtered before proposer selection?

reachability.notes: If inactive validators are included in the selection pool, they could be chosen as proposers but would be unable to produce blocks.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the get_beacon_proposer_indices function in the consensus layer implementation. 2. Verify that get_active_validator_indices(state, epoch) is called before any proposer selection logic. 3. Trace the validator set used in proposer computation and confirm it originates from get_active_validator_indices. 4. Attempt to inject an inactive validator (exited, slashed, or not yet activated) into the selection pool and verify it is rejected. 5. Write a test case that creates a state with mixed active/inactive validators and asserts only active validators appear in proposer indices.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-38, Invariant: FULU-SG006-INV-001, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Impact: Including inactive validators would cause missed slots when selected as proposers, degrading network liveness.
